methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QunaSys, a Series B quantum computing company founded by Tennin Yan, operates within a sector comprising 3 public and 8 private comparable firms, including well-capitalized peers such as IonQ, Quantinuum, and D-Wave Quantum. The company has raised $33M to date.

Valuation was derived using stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $33M total raised as a baseline proxy. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $63M. A peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow quantum computing cohort—while 7 same-sector peers exist, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage competitor met filter criteria, below the minimum threshold of 3 required for reliable triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $72M (high), centered on a $63M median. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust peer set for cross-validation; reliance on a single method limits confidence in this range. The stage-progression approach itself assumes QunaSys follows typical venture step-up patterns, which may not hold in an emerging, heterogeneous sector where capital efficiency and technical differentiation vary substantially across competitors. Additional clarity on revenue trajectory and competitive position relative to the named peer group would strengthen conviction in this valuation.
